session.assistant:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document. Let me compile all the findings:
## COMPREHENSIVE EXPLORATION REPORT: JSON Commands, MCP Integration, and One-Shot Patterns
### DOCUMENTATION STRUCTURE
#### Key Documentation Files Found: 1. **`docs/learned/cli/json-command-decorator.md`** - Complete reference for `@json_command` decorator 2. **`docs/learned/cli/adding-json-to-commands.md`** - Step-by-step guide for adding JSON to commands 3. **`docs/learned/integrations/mcp-integration.md`** - MCP server configuration and tool discovery 4. **`docs/learned/cli/tripwires.md`** - 224 tripwires covering CLI patterns including JSON commands 5. **`docs/learned/testing/tripwires.md`** - 148 tripwires for testing patterns 6. **`docs/learned/planning/one-shot-workflow.md`** - Complete one-shot workflow documentation 7. **`docs/learned/erk/codespace-machine-types.md`** - Codespace machine configuration documentation
---
### CORE DECORATOR IMPLEMENTATIONS
#### 1. **@json_command Decorator** (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/json_command.py`)
**Purpose**: Adds `--json` and `--schema` flags to Click commands with JSON error serialization, stdin input mapping, and schema generation.
**Key Components**: - `JsonCommandMeta` dataclass: stores metadata (exclude_json_input, required_json_input, output_types) - `json_command()` function: Two forms - bare (`@json_command`) or parameterized - `read_stdin_json()`: Reads JSON from stdin when piped - `emit_json()`: Emits JSON success results with `success: True` - `emit_json_result()`: Calls `result.to_json_dict()` or falls back to `dataclasses.asdict()`
**Decorator Parameters**: ```python @json_command( exclude_json_input=frozenset({"repo_id"}), # Params not from JSON stdin required_json_input=frozenset({"prompt"}), # Required JSON fields output_types=(PrListResult, PrViewResult), # Result types for schema ) ```
**Error Handling**: - Catches `click.ClickException` with `error_type` attribute - Serializes as: `{"success": false, "error_type": "...", "message": "..."}` - Three error categories: validation errors, command errors, passthrough (SystemExit)
#### 2. **@mcp_exposed Decorator** (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/mcp_exposed.py`)
**Purpose**: Marks `@json_command` decorated commands for automatic MCP tool registration.
**Key Components**: - `McpMeta` dataclass: stores name and description - `mcp_exposed()` decorator: Attaches metadata to Click command - `discover_mcp_commands()`: Walks CLI tree to find all `@mcp_exposed` commands
**Usage**: ```python @mcp_exposed( name="one_shot", description="Submit a task for fully autonomous remote execution" ) @json_command(...) @click.command("one-shot") ```
**Decorator Stack Order** (outermost to innermost): 1. `@mcp_exposed(...)` — MCP exposure 2. `@json_command(...)` — JSON infrastructure 3. `@click.command(...)` — Click command 4. `@click.option(...)` — Options/arguments 5. `@click.pass_obj` — Context passing
---
### TEST FILES INVENTORY
#### Command Tests (tests/commands/): 1. **`test_one_shot.py`** - Core one-shot command tests 2. **`test_one_shot_json.py`** - JSON input/output tests for one-shot 3. **`test_one_shot_dispatch.py`** - Dispatch behavior tests 4. **`test_one_shot_remote_dispatch.py`** - Remote dispatch via RemoteGitHub 5. **`test_plan_one_shot.py`** - Objective plan one-shot tests
#### CLI Unit Tests (tests/unit/cli/): 1. **`test_json_command.py`** - Meta test validating `output_types` matches return annotations 2. **`test_pr_json.py`** - JSON serialization tests for PrListResult and PrViewResult 3. **`test_mcp_cli_sync.py`** - MCP parity tests (discovery, schema validation, no orphans)
#### Core Tests: 1. **`test_llm_json.py`** - LLM JSON handling tests
---
### WORKING EXAMPLES
#### **One-Shot Command** (`src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py`)
Complete working example with both decorators:
```python @mcp_exposed( name="one_shot", description="Submit a task for fully autonomous remote execution." ) @json_command( exclude_json_input=frozenset({"file_path"}), required_json_input=frozenset({"prompt"}), output_types=(OneShotDispatchResult, OneShotDryRunResult), ) @click.command("one-shot") @click.argument("prompt", required=False, default=None) # ... options ... @click.pass_obj def one_shot( ctx: ErkContext, *, json_mode: bool, prompt: str | None, # ... other params ... ) -> OneShotDispatchResult | OneShotDryRunResult: """Submit a task for fully autonomous remote execution.""" # ... implementation ... return result ```
#### **Result Classes with JSON Serialization**
`OneShotDispatchResult`: ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class OneShotDispatchResult: pr_number: int run_id: str branch_name: str pr_url: str run_url: str
def to_json_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]: return { "dry_run": False, "pr_number": self.pr_number, "run_id": self.run_id, "branch_name": self.branch_name, "pr_url": self.pr_url, "run_url": self.run_url, } ```
`PrViewResult` with custom serialization: ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class PrViewResult: plan_id: str title: str state: str url: str | None labels: list[str] assignees: list[str] created_at: str updated_at: str objective_id: int | None branch: str | None header_fields: dict[str, object] body: str | None
def to_json_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]: result: dict[str, Any] = { "plan_id": self.plan_id, "title": self.title, "state": self.state, "url": self.url, # ... other fields ... } if self.body is not None: result["body"] = self.body # Conditionally include fields return result ```
---
### MCP INTEGRATION ARCHITECTURE
**MCP Tools Workflow**:
1. **Tool Registration**: Commands decorated with both `@json_command` and `@mcp_exposed` are auto-discovered 2. **Schema Generation**: Click parameters automatically converted to JSON Schema 3. **Tool Execution**: Each tool wraps the command, filters unset parameters, pipes as JSON to CLI
**Three Registered Tools**: - `plan_list` - delegates to `erk exec dash-data` - `plan_view` - delegates to `erk exec get-plan-info` - `one_shot` - delegates to `erk one-shot --json`
**Parity Tests** (tests/unit/cli/test_mcp_cli_sync.py): - Every `@mcp_exposed` command is registered as an MCP tool - Tool schema matches Click-derived schema - No orphaned tools without corresponding `@mcp_exposed` commands - Every `@mcp_exposed` command has `@json_command`
---
### KEY PATTERNS AND CONVENTIONS
#### Result Dataclass Rules: 1. **Always frozen**: `@dataclass(frozen=True)` 2. **Implement `to_json_dict()`**: For custom serialization 3. **Conditionally include fields**: Check `if field is not None` before including 4. **Handle complex types**: Use helper functions like `_serialize_header_fields()` for datetime
#### JSON Input Validation: 1. Keys must match command parameters 2. Keys in `exclude_json_input` are rejected from JSON input 3. Keys in `required_json_input` must be present and non-None 4. Invalid inputs produce error_type validation errors
#### Error Handling: - Use `UserFacingCliError(message, error_type="custom_type")` for expected failures - Errors serialize as JSON with success=false when `--json` is active - SystemExit and non-Click exceptions pass through unchanged
---
### TESTING PATTERNS
#### JSON Command Tests: ```python # Test JSON output result = runner.invoke(cli, ["command", "--json"], obj=ctx) data = json.loads(result.output) assert data["success"] is True
# Test JSON input from stdin result = runner.invoke(cli, ["command", "--json"], obj=ctx, input='{"prompt": "value"}')
# Test JSON error output result = runner.invoke(cli, ["command", "--json"], obj=ctx) assert result.exit_code == 1 data = json.loads(result.output) assert data["error_type"] == "validation_error" ```
#### MCP Parity Tests: - Walk CLI tree for `@mcp_exposed` commands - Create MCP server and call `list_tools()` - Verify command_input_schema matches for each tool - Ensure all commands have `_json_command_meta`
---
### NO EXISTING MACHINE_COMMAND OR MACHINE_SCHEMA
**Search Results**: No files matching `machine_command`, `machine_schema`, `MachineCommand`, or `MachineSchema` exist in the codebase. The codespace documentation references "machine types" (hardw are configuration), not a code pattern.
---
### CRITICAL TRIPWIRES (from docs/learned/cli/tripwires.md)
1. **Decorator Stack Order**: `@mcp_exposed` MUST be above `@json_command` (not below) 2. **output_types Validation**: Test validates output_types against return annotation; mismatches fail CI 3. **JSON Input Handling**: All keys must match command parameters; reject unknown keys 4. **Error Types**: Use `AgentCliError(message, error_type=...)` for proper JSON serialization 5. **Result Serialization**: Call `to_json_dict()` or fallback to `dataclasses.asdict()` 6. **No Human Output in JSON Mode**: When `--json` is active, stdout is only JSON; user output goes to stderr
---
### CLI TESTING PATTERNS (from tests/commands/AGENTS.md)
- Use `erk_isolated_fs_env(runner)` for filesystem context - Use `env.build_context()` helper to construct test context with fakes - Never use `monkeypatch` or `@patch` - use gateway fakes instead - Use `CliRunner` with dependency injection for all command tests - Test both success and error paths
---
### FILE PATHS (Absolute)
**Core Implementation**: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/json_command.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/mcp_exposed.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py`
**Test Files**: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/test_pr_json.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/test_mcp_cli_sync.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py`
**Documentation**: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/json-command-decorator.md` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/adding-json-to-commands.md` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/integrations/mcp-integration.md` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/one-shot-workflow.md` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/tripwires.md` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/tripwires.md`
---
This exploration provides a complete picture of the JSON command, MCP, and one-shot command patterns in the erk codebase. All patterns are documented, tested, and follow consistent architectural conventions.
